page.content: The tables present critical information regarding diarrhoea grading and biomarker levels. In Table 1, the headers include "Diarrhoea grade" and "Definition/timing," with the definitions ranging from "Increase <4 stools/day over baseline; mild increase in ostomy output" for Grade 1, "Increase 4–6 stools/day over baseline; moderate increase in ostomy output" for Grade 2, "Increase ≥7 stools/day; incontinence; hospitalization indicated; severe increase in ostomy output limiting self-care" for Grade 3, to "Life-threatening consequences; urgent intervention indicated" for Grade 4. The pre-chemotherapy management follows as "Observe but continue to chemotherapy" for Grade 1, "Stop, stagger or reduce curcumin, continue chemotherapy when AE grade <2" for Grade 2, and "Stop curcumin, continue with chemotherapy when AE grade <2" for Grades 3 and 4. During chemotherapy, protocols include "Observe but continue chemotherapy" for Grade 1, "Reduce or stagger curcumin" for Grade 2, and "Stop curcumin, continue reduced chemotherapy as per protocol" for Grade 4. Forty-eight hours post one curcumin dose reduction, the same actions apply as during chemotherapy. Table 2 details biomarkers: Aβ1–42, ng/L shows 188 (134–208) at baseline, 202 (113–207) at 3 months, p Value vs. BL is 1.0, and 183 (123–206) at 6 months with p Value vs. BL at 0.27; t-tau, ng/L is 128 (70–235), 120 (64–256), 1.0, 113 (76–187), 0.46; p-tau, ng/L is 36 (18–76), 42 (35–48), 1.0, 46 (33–55), 0.72; NFL, ng/L is 301 (57–557), 1959 (846–2632), 0.07, 718 (236–758), 0.07; GFAP, ng/L is 586 (395–891), 612 (387–943), 0.72, 589 (353–833), 1.0; ChAT activity, nmol/ml/minute is 373 (317–447), 367 (279–414), 0.14, 358 (337–410), 0.47; AChE activity, nmol/ml/minute is 12 (9–22), 10 (9–21), 0.07, 10 (9–23), 0.27.
